question: What are the resources, data, or materials that are put into a system to be processed? [cite: 58]
Which system component represents the activities or operations that transform inputs into outputs? [cite: 61]
What system component encompasses everything outside the system that can influence its operation and performance? [cite: 75, 76]
Which of the following defines the limits of a system and differentiates it from its surroundings? [cite: 77, 78]
What type of system is characterized by having all inputs and outputs contained within it, meaning it does not interact with its environment? [cite: 83, 84]
In the context of Information Systems, what are communication systems that connect hardware components and allow data sharing called? [cite: 107, 108]
During which SDLC phase are project initiation, feasibility analysis, and resource allocation performed? [cite: 118]
What are the expected deliverables at the end of the SDLC Design phase? [cite: 129]
options: Design documents, data models, UI/UX prototypes
Source code, database schema, developed modules
Test plans, test cases, defect reports
Project charter, feasibility study report, project plan

question: Which SDLC model is most suitable for projects with well-defined requirements and low uncertainty? [cite: 157]
Which SDLC methodology emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and utilizes iterative cycles called sprints? [cite: 231, 232]
Assessing the technical, financial, and operational viability of proposed systems falls under which responsibility of a System Analyst? [cite: 247]
What is the primary goal of requirements gathering in the SDLC? [cite: 278]
options: To ensure the final system meets users' requirements and provides value to the organization
To write the source code for the new software implementation
To deploy the new system to the production environment safely
To perform integration and unit testing on individual modules

question: Which requirement elicitation technique involves a facilitated group setting to generate ideas, discuss requirements, and build consensus collaboratively? [cite: 339]
What is a notable demerit of using questionnaires for requirement gathering? [cite: 336]
options: Lack of opportunity for clarification or follow-up questions
High cost compared to interviews in terms of resources
Inability to gather data from a large and diverse group
It requires bringing all stakeholders into a single room

question: What process is used to establish links between requirements and other SDLC artifacts, such as design documents and test cases, to ensure comprehensive coverage? [cite: 356]
In Data Flow Diagrams (DFDs), what standardized symbol is used to represent processes? [cite: 382]
A key limitation of Data Flow Diagrams (DFDs) is that they represent a static view of the system. What do they often fail to capture? [cite: 390]
options: Dynamic aspects such as real-time interactions or system behavior over time
The source and destination of data flows
External entities interacting with the boundaries of the system
Data stores where raw information is physically held

question: In an Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD), what represents the properties or characteristics of entities? [cite: 399]
What ERD concept specifies the maximum and minimum number of occurrences of one entity that may be related to another entity? [cite: 401]
In a Use Case Diagram, what represents users, external systems, or other entities that interact with the system? [cite: 417]
What type of relationship in a Use Case Diagram indicates dependencies where one use case expands or relies on the functionality of another? [cite: 421]
In Activity Diagrams, what component shows the flow of control between activities, indicating the sequence in which actions are executed? [cite: 436]
In Activity Diagrams, what represents points where the flow of control may diverge based on certain conditions or criteria? [cite: 437]
Which database normalization form refines the Third Normal Form (3NF) by eliminating all non-trivial functional dependencies? [cite: 461]
The SQL commands GRANT and REVOKE belong to which Basic Component of SQL? [cite: 493, 494]
Which SQL component is used to manage database transactions, incorporating commands like COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and SAVEPOINT? [cite: 494, 495]
Which interface design principle dictates that design elements should suggest their function (e.g., buttons should look clickable)? [cite: 539, 540]
Which interface design principle ensures that the layout and design elements are uniform, so users do not have to relearn how to use different parts of an application? [cite: 525, 526]
Which usability principle focuses on making the system easy to use again after a period of not using it, without having to relearn the interface? [cite: 563]
What is a major disadvantage of using the Waterfall methodology for software development? [cite: 591, 592]
options: Late discovery of issues since testing happens only after implementation is completed
It requires daily stand-up meetings which exhaust developer resources
It easily accommodates spontaneous scope changes, leading to project creep
There is no structured plan, leading to chaotic implementation

question: According to the key principles of the Agile methodology, what is prioritized over comprehensive documentation? [cite: 596, 597]
What coding technique breaks down software code into distinct components, each responsible for a specific task? [cite: 621]
Implementing mechanisms like try-except blocks in Python to prevent application crashes is an example of which coding technique? [cite: 625, 626]
What type of testing involves re-testing software after changes have been made to ensure that existing functionalities are not affected? [cite: 652]
Which integration testing approach starts from the highest-level modules, using stubs or mock objects to simulate lower-level modules? [cite: 689]
What refers to the set of activities designed to ensure that development processes are adequate, preventing defects and ensuring a system will meet its objectives? [cite: 655, 656]
Which deployment strategy involves maintaining two identical production environments to ensure zero downtime and enable quick rollbacks? [cite: 745, 748]
Which deployment strategy releases the new version of an application to a small subset of users initially to monitor its performance before a full rollout? [cite: 751, 752]
What deployment strategy runs two different versions of an application simultaneously to different user groups to compare user acceptance and conversion rates? [cite: 757, 758]
What type of user training consists of traditional classroom-style instruction led by a teacher, either in person or virtually? [cite: 776]
Fixing errors and bugs that are identified after the software is already in use by end-users is classified as what type of system maintenance? [cite: 817]
Modifying software to keep it compatible with a new operating system version or a new third-party service is an example of what type of maintenance? [cite: 821, 822]
Subversion (SVN) is provided as an example of which type of Version Control System? [cite: 866]
In Version Control terminology, what is a request to merge changes from one branch into another, which is often accompanied by a code review process? [cite: 876]
In a Version Control System, what provides a parallel version of the codebase, allowing for independent development of features or experiments? [cite: 872]
